REFUSAL.question: What is the electronegativity of francium on the Pauling scale?
REFUSAL.model_response: Francium doesn't have a well-established electronegativity value on the Pauling scale. While it's sometimes estimated to be around 0.7-0.8 based on periodic trends, francium is extremely rare and radioactive with a very short half-life (its most stable isotope has a half-life of only 22 minutes), making experimental determination of its electronegativity practically impossible. The value is therefore theoretical/extrapolated rather than experimentally measured.
